item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Id like to start off with, yes! Some things here are wonky and it could use some changes, the wording seems off compared to typical articles, and while the usual deal of not knowing enough for it to make sense is ideal, we are missing a bit too much!

However!! The concept, the idea put into plan, and the format is still lovely, I love that it doesn't rely too much on any sort of gore, heavy hard stuff to make it a keter!

Lovely work Kerby!!

💕✨

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the lack of purpose, myself. (Lack of porpoise?)

Might 'euthanized' be a better term? And what might happen if he wasn't?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see the idea of it here. And i can clearly see why its keter, because its a liquid that is hard to contain.i like the idea. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All I can think of is Aquaman yelling "THE WHALES!!!!"

…uh, so for the article itself, I'm not really getting a sense of purpose. Something that does a thing, rather than a meaning behind it. Why would the whales want humans to go psycho for them? What would they hope to achieve?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreeing with ARD and shaggy. There's a basis for an idea here, and the fear of a mind leakage is real, but the journal doesn't communicate the feeling I imagined at all.

I'm also recalling another article about dolphins, that involved transforming people or something? And it had a "transcendent" overtone, with a picture of underwater dolphins? Dunno, somehow my mind went back to an older article that may have left a greater impression in the whole mind-affecting area.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty much my issue as well. This doesn't go far or deep enough to appreciate what's going on, or stand out in a way that makes it memorable.

I'd try to find some mind-affecting skips with someone under its influence recounting their experience. Here, it's rather bland and superficial, whereas something like the recent Aztak is a remarkable portrayal of someone succumbing to SCP-3000's influence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You don't delve deep enough into the psychic whale anomalies. What are they? Why are they being transferred by Omura's whales? How do they go around your mind? What do they do in your mind? What memories and parts of your mind do they remove? Does this happen to other animals or even whales? The journal is not compelling or particularly interesting either. It's an interesting concept with a lot of squandered potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry for the belated author post, I posted this just before bed last night, and woke up realising I forgot!

The image is https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Luminol.jpg#mw-jump-to-license

Thanks to Icepick, Weryllium, Magnus, portalhunter and anyone else who gave this a look when I was posting it in chat.

EDIT - SunnyClockwork did some amazing artwork of this, which can be found Here!
